reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal claim. The metric space X is taken arbitrary (matching "a metric space X"), the sequence p : ℕ → X and the Cauchy hypothesis CauchySeq p match the informal premise. A subsequence is standardly encoded as p ∘ φ for a strictly monotone φ : ℕ → ℕ, which is exactly what Mathlib uses (cf. tendsto_nhds_of_cauchySeq_of_subseq). The convergence of the subsequence to r is expressed as Tendsto (p ∘ φ) atTop (𝓝 r), and the conclusion Tendsto p atTop (𝓝 r) matches "the full sequence converges to p". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure is preserved.
